title: Echinopsilon eriophorus

The family Capparaceae is an ancient lineage of flowering plants that has undergone significant changes over time. Further research may refine the classification of Echinopsilon eriophorus and its position within the plant kingdom.

intro: Echinopsilon eriophorum, also known as Bugleweed or Lycopus, is a perennial herb belonging to the Lamiaceae family, traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its adaptogenic and anti-anxiety effects, and is used to treat conditions such as insomnia, hypertension, and hyperthyroidism. Bioactive constituents of Echinopsilon eriophorum include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Bugleweed is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its effects and treat various health conditions.
